prompt: Write a short poem (8–16 lines) about the feeling of finishing a long project. Any form you like.
response:
Quenched by the satisfaction of labor's end,
I stand in the silence, marveling at the bend
In my particular path, a vision gleaming bright.
The certitude blossoms: all has kindled right.

Embracing the stillness, I feel eternity coil
Within each shining thread of this hallowed toil,
Each tapestry stitched fast, every final measure met.
Time's eons seem swallowed in a single, endless pet.

To residential streets and sunlit squares roam,
A hero bo cycle, out of an appreciated home.
Observe the volumes breathe and bask in light,
